description: Nine-run sequencing scan on the no-distance edge-graph control branch. The graph update step is combinatorial and seed-focused, derived from incidence-mismatch accumulation on adjacency and graph-shell support refinements, while topology labels are tracked with the fixed projected-transverse measurement vocabulary from Paper 24.1.
architecture_notice: This branch keeps graph size, adjacency and graph-shell kernels, the clustered / corridor / triad representative families, the projected-transverse sector, and the shell-weight measurement vocabulary fixed. Only the sequencing rule changes.
baseline_notice: C0.5 replaces the assumed packet-evolution clock with a combinatorial support-update rule. Sequencing is derived from incidence-mismatch accumulation, kernel-update consistency, and protected topology-stability blocks.

base_seed_reference.selection_note: same frozen packet family and representative seeds used in Paper 24.1

sequencing_modes.label: Adjacency sequencing
sequencing_modes.definition: Advance the survivor support using adjacency refinement only and track the resulting persistence-class set.

sequencing_modes.label: Graph-shell sequencing
sequencing_modes.definition: Advance the survivor support using graph-shell refinement only and track the resulting persistence-class set.

sequencing_modes.label: Protected alternating sequencing
sequencing_modes.definition: Choose the lower-mismatch adjacency or graph-shell refinement at each step while the PASS-like protected topology-stability rule blocks merge events.

runs.notes: Adjacency sequencing on the clustered composite anchor.
Adjacency sequencing on the counter-propagating corridor.
Adjacency sequencing on the phase-ordered symmetric triad.
Graph-shell sequencing on the clustered composite anchor.
Graph-shell sequencing on the counter-propagating corridor.
Graph-shell sequencing on the phase-ordered symmetric triad.
Protected alternating sequencing on the clustered composite anchor.
Protected alternating sequencing on the counter-propagating corridor.
Protected alternating sequencing on the phase-ordered symmetric triad.
